rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY** CAROLEE LUBIN SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/21. The proof of service filed on 12/1/20 is incomplete as to the date of mailing and is incompletely verified. The starting number of shares of the funds at the beginning of the second year of the biennial account (when there is no need to prepare two separate accounts), or on the second page 2 of 17 of the account, shows the incorrect number of shares, and assets on hand as of 8/31/20 still show shares on hand of the funds that were liquidated. Why was there an overdraft fee of $35 on 2/19/20? The petition does not disclose any information about the beneficiary or her needs. Was she able to travel alone on the cruise to Alaska in 2019? Why were medical transport costs incurred in June 2020? There are numerous time entries for services as care manager that appear to be duplicated in time entries as trustee. It is unclear why services as care manager include banking, bill paying, and tax-related time. (Not all entries have been reviewed in detail yet.)

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Reserve

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NORMAN LLOYD PETERSEN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/22/2021 to allow a court investigator's report on the related conservatorship to be completed. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 22 of the petition: The bond appears insufficient based on the ending value of this account. The account will not be approved until an additional bond is on file. LRSF 14.16(A)(2). 2) Summary of Account: "Account Current" box was not checked. 3) Please provide a copy of the divorce settlement agreement referred to on Schedule A. 4) Schedule B: No original closing escrow statement is on file. Probate Code section 2620(c)(4). 5) Schedule C: i) Was the conservator of the person authorized to receive "care management fees" reported on Schedule C? ii) All mortgage payments were entirely allocated to income. Were they all interest only payments without any principal reduction? iii) "Interest to 12/18/19" paid on 12/17/2019 was allocated to principal instead of income. 6) Schedules E & F: i) No original bank statement verifying the ending cash balance is on file. LRSF 14.35(D) & 14.60(D). See also Probate Code section 2620(c)(1). ii) The Court does not approve a single institution holding funds exceeding the FDIC limit. Please provide a proof that the excess funds have been segregated and held at additional institutions. 7) Item 1 of notice of the hearing filed on 11/30/2020 did not indicate the petitioner's fiduciary capacity. 8) The verification was signed without indicating the petitioner's fiduciary capacity. 9) The petitioner's fee declaration must separate his services as conservator and services as trustee. Each entry must also show the amount charged. LRSF 14.53. 10) The petitioner spent 4.2 hours on 03/28/2019 to prepare conservatorship accounting, but Schedule C shows disbursements exceeding $1,000 to a third party for "accounting services" on 06/27/2019 and 09/20/2019. Should those disbursements be deducted from the petitioner's fees? 11) Please provide copies of "Petersen Dissolution Judgment" and "Deeds of Trust from 2015" mentioned in the petitioner's entries on 07/03/2019. 12) The attorney's fee declaration shows the attorney and paralegal spent 20 hours on accounting. Please address the productivity of the time spent. 13) Please provide a copy of a receipt showing the $729.61 filing fee on 07/24/2019. 14) The fee declarations of the petitioner and his attorney both describe services related to partition action. Please explain the relevance and benefit of those services to this trust. *All future pleadings must comply with LRSF 14.48(B)(3) and include the related conservatorship case number in parentheses.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVES DENIS MOUISSET
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Ordinarily, when assets are sold, a personal representative discloses the amount received from the sale(s). In light of the sensitive items in this estate, does the Court wish to see evidence of the sale, surrender, or donation of the firearms? 2) Probate Code Section 9202(b) requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation or confined in any county or city jail, road camp, industrial farm, or other local correctional facility. The administrator states only that the sole heir is not currently so confined and that he resides in France. 3) The clerk has confirmed that only one payment was received on 8/3/18 for certified copies. 4) The supplement filed on 12/7/20 increased the costs to be reimbursed to the attorney; this amounts to an amendment, which requires notice. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53(a).] Legitimate closing costs can be paid out of the reserve. Without notice, the higher amount cannot be granted at this time. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LYNN MCKANNAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/22/2021 for failure to file a response to the Examiner Notes that were emailed 11/12/2020. Issues: 1) Petitioner did not check a required box on page 3 under #5.a. Please indicate whether there is a (3) registered domestic partner or (4) no registered domestic partner. 2) Please explain why on page 3 under #6 Petitioner checked box b when Decedent was survived by an alleged spouse and alleged daughter. #6 says (Complete if decedent was survived by (1) a spouse or registered domestic partner but no issue (only a or b apply), or (2) no spouse, registered domestic partner, or issue.) 3) The petition alleges Petitioner is Decedent's spouse and Decedent has a daughter. Please explain why on page 3 under #7 Petitioner checked box A. #7 says (Complete only if no spouse or issue survived decedent.) 4) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANNE L. GRAFENBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (that may be addressed at hearing): 1) Attorney Carolyn Espey opened this estate and was issued letters using the incorrect date of death of Decedent of December 5, 2018. The petition for final distribution and death certificate show the date of death as December 6, 2018. 2) Attorney Carolyn Espey did not seek court authority under LRSF 14.88 C before paying an attorney in the UK to collect funds at Nat West Bank. Executor requests the court to retroactively authorize this payment and reimburse Carolyn Espey $2,470.00 for additional payment made. Court to determine whether to require payment of interest and/or surcharge the Executor. 3) Sellers statement has an entry attorney fees/costs of $427.50. Caroline Espey alleges Celink refused to remove the attorney fees and costs which were paid out of escrow.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARY MARGARET ISHAM REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/22/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) Were the tangible personal items distributed after 02/03/2019? If so, Schedule 1 must list the items. If distributed during this account period, a separate distribution schedule must be filed. 2) Schedules E & F must list the wooden chair proposed to be distributed to Kate Stearns and other items distribtued or otherwise retained after this account period. 3) Separate notice must be given to Shira Fine and Jen Garrido. 4) Notice of the hearing filed on 01/05/2021 sets forth the past hearing date. 5) Please identify the heirs within the meaning of Article 4 of the governing trust. 6) Please identify the alternate charity receiving the cash gift to Unidosporpuertorico.com. 7) Please provide a date of distribution of each specific cash gift.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF DONTE SASUKE MAK HAYES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) No inventory and appraisal and notice of how to file objection have been filed; and 2) No order waiving accounts has been issued. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VIRGIL PRATER AND BETTY E. PRATER 1991 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/21. Prior to a full review of the petition, the following must be addressed: 1) Petitioner alleges that the principal place of trust administration is San Francisco County, but Trustee resides in Los Altos. In a supplemental declaration verified by the petitioner, please allege where trust administration takes place. Please note that the principal place of administration is not necessarily where settlors resided or where assets of the trust located. 2) The wrong Judicial Council Form was used for notice, which must be on DE-115. Filed stamped copy of all pleadings filed to be provided at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY M. SMITH 1992 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine The Validity Of The 2014 Amendment To The Shirley M. Smith Trust, For Removal Of Trustee, For Accounting, For Imposition
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/22/2021. Issues: 1) The courtesy copy of the petition received by Examiner is missing pages of the trusts. Please provide Examiner with a complete endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copy of the 09/04/2020 petition. 2) Petitioner has not provided notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. 3) Please provide evidence in support of the petition. 4) Please explain page 2 lines 4-14 of the petition. 5) Please provide legal authority in support of page 7 lines 16-17. 6) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIO V. MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/21. (No earlier availability.) No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A., and 14.60 J.] If the whereabouts of interested parties are unknown, a declaration must be filed describing efforts made to locate the persons entitled to notice. If, after diligent search, such persons cannot be located, an ex parte petition must be filed to request that notice be dispensed with to such persons. Bond must be for the full value of the estate's property, not an amount per year.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTHONY CORBETT RICHMOND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/01/2021. 1) The petition alleges Decedent died 10/31/1988. Please explain why a petition has been filed 32 years later. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) Petition page 2 #3h(2) does not specify permanent address. 4) Petition page 3 #5a boxes are blank and the boxes must be checked for (1) or (2) and (3) or (4) and (5) or (6) and (7) or (8). 5) A hard copy of the proposed order and response to the Examiner notes must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 4pm on January 18, 2021. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]
